data.title: The Prognosis of Lipid Reprogramming With the HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibitor, Rosuvastatin, in Castrated Egyptian Prostate Cancer Patients
data.brief_summary:
Aim: The role of surgical castration and rosuvastatin treatment on lipid profile and lipid metabolism related markers was evaluated for their prognostic significance in metastatic prostate cancer (mPC) patients.

Methods: A total of 70 newly diagnosed castrated mPC patients treated with castration were recruited and divided into two groups: Group I included 30 patients and served as control (statin non-users) while group II included 40 patients treated with Rosuvastatin (20 mg/day) for 6 months
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Naïve newly diagnosed with metastatic prostate cancer
* Age ≥ 50 years.
* No psychological or geographical barriers for regular follow up of the patients.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Age \< 50 years.
* psychological or geographical barriers for regular follow up of the patients.
